history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Forward from here (to row 02 column C) is floor `.` and safe (not lava). I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column C facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. Forward from here is row 02 column D which is a floor (`.`). Moving forward keeps me in safe cells and gets me closer to aligning with the path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column D facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I need to move down, but the cell in front of me (row 02 column E) is a wall. So I should turn left (counter-clockwise) to face down, then move forward toward the open path that leads downward around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column D facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I can’t move forward because row 01 column D is a wall (`#`). From here, the safe move toward the goal is to turn right so I can move into row 02 column E, which will then let me work down toward the goal avoiding lava (`L` at row 03 column C and row 04 column C). So I will turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column D facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 04 column D. I should move forward if the next cell (row 02 column E) is safe, but it is `#` (a wall). So I need to change direction: from facing right to facing down, I can turn right once (clockwise).
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column D facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D. The cell in front (row 03 column D) is floor `.` so it’s safe—move forward to continue toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column D facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 04 column D, which is directly one step forward. That cell is `G` (safe), so I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
